reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Japanese, preserving code blocks, MDX components, frontmatter, etc. Must keep frontmatter keys same, translate only reader-facing values: title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Also language field? Not present. Should we add language? Not required unless exists. Keep other metadata unchanged. We need to translate the prose, headings, blockquotes, etc. Keep code unchanged. Also need to prefix relative URLs with ../ . The original relative URLs: "./hero-wide.webp", "./square-200.webp", "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg", "./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp". Need to prefix with ../ . So change to "../hero-wide.webp" etc. Also the link in blockquote: https://datatracker.ietf.org/... is absolute, keep unchanged. The link in blockquote with class "ai-response inset" includes a line break
, keep unchanged. Also need to translate the blockquote text. Also need to translate the markdown headings: "#", "##", "###", etc. Keep same level. Also need to translate the blockquote class "inset" unchanged. Also need to translate the MDX component? There's no MDX component aside from blockquote tags. Those are HTML tags; we keep them. Also need to translate the image alt text? The image tags are markdown: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg). Alt text is "the parts of a LLM connection string". Should translate that alt text. Also the other image alt: "a messy env var drawer". Translate. Also need to translate the footnote note: {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. If you are pedantic enough to actually care about that distinction, please go touch grass. */}. That's a comment block, keep as is but translate the comment text? It's a comment, not rendered. It's part of source. Should we translate? It's not reader-facing? It's a comment for developers, but still part of article. Probably keep as is? The instruction: translate only reader-facing prose. Comments are not displayed, but they are part of source. Might be okay to leave unchanged. But could translate for consistency. However it's a comment, not displayed, but still part of source. I'd keep unchanged to avoid altering code. Now produce translation. Let's translate each paragraph. First blockquote: **Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](...). Translate: **更新:** 本記事は `llm://` URI スキームの [Internet-Draft](...) に繋がっています。 Now the opening paragraph: "Remember the bad old days when connecting to a database meant juggling a miscellaneous grab-bag of environment variables?" Translate: "データベース接続の際に、環境変数の寄せ集めをいちいち扱わなければならなかったあの暗い時代を覚えていますか？" Continue. "It was a tower of delicate config. `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME`... or wait, was it `DB_USERNAME`? Is it `DB_PASS` or `DB_PWD`? Do I need the `PG_*` prefixes this time? And where the hell does the timeout setting go?" Translate: "それは繊細な設定の塔でした。`DB_HOST`、`DB_PORT`、`DB_USER`、`DB_PASSWORD`、`DB_NAME`…いや、`DB_USERNAME`だったかな？`DB_PASS`か`DB_PWD`か？今回は`PG_*`プレフィックスが必要か？そしてタイムアウト設定はどこに入れるんだ？" "It was a fragile house of cards, ready to topple your production build because you forgot to capitalize `HOST`." Translate: "それは脆いカードの塔で、`HOST`の大文字化を忘れただけで本番ビルドが崩壊しかねませんでした。" "Then, someone had the brilliant idea to just use a URL¹:" Translate: "そこで、誰かが「URLだけで済ませよう」という brilliant なアイデアを思いつきました¹：" Code block unchanged. "One string. Everything you need. Universally parseable. Portable. Dare I say... beautiful?" Translate: "1 本の文字列で、必要なものはすべて。汎用的にパース可能で、ポータブル。敢えて言うなら…美しい。" "So why are we treating LLMs like it's 1999?" Translate: "それなのに、なぜ LLM を 1999 年のまま扱っているのでしょうか？" Heading "## The Env Var Explosion" translate: "## 環境変数の爆発" Paragraph: "Right now, my `.env` file looks like a graveyard of abandoned API keys. `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `MISTRAL_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`. And don't get me started on Azure—you need an endpoint, a deployment name, an API version, and a key just to say "hello"." Translate: "現在、私の `.env` ファイルは放棄された API キーの墓場のようです。`OPENAI_API_KEY`、`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`、`MISTRAL_API_KEY`、`GROQ_API_KEY`。Azure については語りません—「こんにちは」と言うだけでもエンドポイント、デプロイ名、API バージョン、キーが必要です。" "It's not just ugly; it's friction. Every time I want to swap a model or test a new provider, I'm rewriting initialization code, hunting down documentation for specific parameter names, and adding three more lines to my environment config." Translate: "見た目が悪いだけでなく、摩擦が増えます。モデルを切り替えたり新しいプロバイダーをテストしたりするたびに、初期化コードを書き直し、パラメータ名のドキュメントを探し、環境設定にさらに数行足さなければなりません。" "What if we just... ~~stole~~ borrowed the DB URL idea?" Translate: "もし私たちが… ~~盗む~~ DB URL の考え方を借りたらどうでしょうか？" Heading "## Introducing LLM Connection Strings" translate: "## LLM 接続文字列の導入" Paragraph: "Imagine configuring your entire model interface with a single line:" translate: "モデルインターフェース全体を 1 行で設定できると想像してみてください：" Code block unchanged. Then "---\n
\n\n### Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" translate heading: "### LLM 接続文字列の構成要素" Image alt text: "the parts of a LLM connection string" translate: "LLM 接続文字列の構成要素" Update image path: "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg" -> "../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg" Paragraph: "The scheme is `llm://`. The host is the provider's API base URL. The path is the model name. And query parameters handle all the runtime options that usually clutter your code." Translate: "スキームは `llm://` です。ホストはプロバイダーの API ベース URL。パスはモデル名です。クエリパラメータは、通常コードを散らかすすべてのランタイムオプションを処理します。" Heading "## Need auth? Great, add it." translate: "## 認証が必要ですか？ では追加しましょう。" Paragraph: "Just like `postgres://`, we can bake authentication right in:" translate: "`postgres://` と同様に、認証情報を URL に埋め込むことができます：" Code block unchanged. Note: "*Note: Yes, putting credentials in URLs can be a security risk if you're pasting them into public logs. But modern logging services are pretty good at scrubbing these patterns, and honestly, are you treating your `.env` file much better? Verify, sanitize, and use with caution.*" Translate: "*注: 確かに、URL に認証情報を入れると公開ログに貼り付けた際のセキュリティリスクがあります。しかし、最新のロギングサービスはこのようなパターンをかなり除去してくれますし、正直に言えば `.env` ファイルをもっと丁寧に扱っていますか？検証し、サニタイズし、注意して使用してください。*" Heading "## Resiliency? Why the hell not." translate: "## レジリエンス？ なぜだめなのですか。" Paragraph: "Many database libraries support round-robin failover by specifying multiple hosts. Why shouldn't our AI agents have the same reliability?" translate: "多くのデータベースライブラリは複数ホストを指定してラウンドロビンフェイルオーバーをサポートしています。我々の AI エージェントも同様の信頼性を持つべきでは？" Code block unchanged. Paragraph: "That `s` in `llms://` isn't a typo. It's plural. If `primary.gpt` hangs, the client automatically retries `backup.gpt`. No complex router logic required." Translate: "`llms://` の `s` はタイプミスではなく、複数形です。`primary.gpt` がハングした場合、クライアントは自動的に `backup.gpt` にリトライします。複雑なルーターロジックは不要です。" Blockquote: "
One string with everything from your **auth** to your **endpoint** to your **hyperparameters**.
" Translate inner text: "認証情報からエンドポイント、ハイパーパラメータまで、すべてを 1 つの文字列で。" Keep markup. Heading "## Alternative Formats" translate: "## 代替フォーマット" Paragraph: "I'm not married to `llm://`. The specific scheme matters less than the standard itself." translate: "`llm://` に固執しているわけではありません。具体的なスキームよりも標準そのものが重要です。" Paragraph: "I could imagine a world where we use provider-specific schemes for brevity, while keeping the standard structure:" translate: "簡潔さのためにプロバイダー固有のスキームを使い、標準構造は維持する世界を想像できます：" Code block unchanged. Paragraph: "Regardless of the exact syntax, the core benefits are undeniable:" translate: "正確な構文はどうであれ、核心的な利点は否定できません：" List items translate: 1. "**Portability:** Copy & paste your entire config from a local script to a cloud worker." -> "**ポータビリティ:** ローカルスクリプトからクラウドワーカーへ、設定全体をコピー＆ペーストできます。" 2. "**CLI Friendly:** Pass a single argument to your scripts. `my-agent --model \"llm://...\"` beats `my-agent --model gpt-4 --temp 0.7 --key $KEY --host ...`." -> "**CLI フレンドリー:** スクリプトに単一の引数を渡すだけです。`my-agent --model \"llm://...\"` は `my-agent --model gpt-4 --temp 0.7 --key $KEY --host ...` よりも遥かにシンプルです。" 3. "**Language Agnostic:** Every programming language has a robust URL parser. We get validation, parsing, and sanitization for free." -> "**言語非依存:** すべてのプログラミング言語に堅牢な URL パーサーがあります。検証、パース、サニタイズが自動的に得られます。" Blockquote with class "ai-response inset": "
The database world took decades to figure this out.
Good news, in AI timelines, that's only about half a vibe-year ago.
" Translate inner text: "データベース界がこれを解明するのに数十年かかりました。
良いニュースです、AI のタイムラインではそれはたった半年ほど前のことです。" Heading "## The Verdict" translate: "## 結論" Paragraph: "We don't need another complex configuration standard or a new YAML-based manifest file. We just need to use the one tool that's been working for the rest of the internet for the last 30 years." translate: "別の複雑な設定標準や新しい YAML マニフェストファイルは必要ありません。過去 30 年間インターネット全体で実績のある、あのツールを使うだけです。" Paragraph: "Let's stop reinventing the wheel and start treating our LLM connections with the same respect we give our databases. Your `.env` file (and your sanity) will thank you." translate: "車輪の再発明はやめて、LLM 接続をデータベースと同等の敬意で扱いましょう。`.env` ファイル（そしてあなたの正気）も感謝するはずです。" Image alt: "a messy env var drawer" translate: "乱雑な環境変数の引き出し" Image path: "./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp" -> "../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp" Comment block: keep unchanged. Now need to adjust frontmatter image paths: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp -> ../hero-wide.webp etc. Also cover_mobile, cover_icon. Also need to add language field? Not required. Now produce final output.
